questions.prompt: A Detect rule that fires on every single occurrence of a denied execution is a good rule because it catches all instances.
questions.explanation: Without an Occurrences threshold or severity tiering, single-event rules drown the on-call rotation in noise. A single denied nmap execution might be a tech doing ops; the same denial 50 times in 10 minutes is the signal worth paging on.

questions.prompt: What's the most useful artefact for designing a new Detect rule?
choices: Real Unified Audit data grouped by relevant fields
questions.explanation: Rules built from real audit data tend to fire on real anomalies. Rules built from imagination fire on legitimate edge cases and miss the actual patterns the customer's environment exhibits.
